title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Virginia Rue confronts George Romax regarding his motive for asking her to pose as a sophisticated British lady for the visiting Canadian, Mr. McGregor. George claims he intends to influence McGregor to support a monarchist restoration in Hesse, specifically proposing Prince Michael O'Paulwich as king. Virginia dismisses this explanation as a lie, noting that the media is already saturated with rumors concerning the O'Paulwich dynasty and the late Nicolas IV. Realizing Virginia is more inquisitive than he anticipated, George attempts to withdraw from the arrangement, but Virginia informs him that she plans to attend a gathering at the Chimney House over the weekend to investigate the "McGrath mystery." After George departs, Virginia calls Miss Elinor Brent to confirm her attendance at the Chimney House.

Meanwhile, at the Bretton Hotel, Anthony McGlass deals with the disappearance of important letters. He suspects a waiter named Giuseppe, who may be working for the "Red Hand League" or Royalists, stole the letters after mistaking them for a memoir. Anthony meets with the hotel manager to report the theft. Although Anthony initially claims only trivial items were taken to avoid a scandal, he eventually admits to the manager that the stolen items are valuable letters. To avoid media attention, the manager agrees not to involve the police. Anthony requests information on Giuseppe, and the manager provides details, noting that Giuseppe is a skilled waiter who has been in Britain for five years.
